channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Four memorable moments from Kamala Harris' CNN town hall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kamala Harris went to a Philadelphia suburb on Wednesday night for a CNN town hall, where she faced questions from undecided voters. The event was arranged after Donald Trump declined to participate in a second debate with her.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beyoncé to campaign with Harris: Sources
item.description: The Grammy-winning artist previously endorsed Harris at the conclusion of the Democratic National Convention in Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: From Denton to Edinburg, Texas cities are spooking their residents and tourists this Halloween
item.description: Denton has renamed itself Halloween, Texas, and has planned 31 days of events to drive up tourism and family entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jayme Lozano Carver And Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida sues Garland for blocking Trump assassination attempt investigation
item.description: Florida sued Attorney General Merrick Garland on Wednesday, saying the Justice Department is improperly blocking the state from investigating the second assassination attempt of former President Trump. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US must weigh 'military action against' North Korean forces if invading Ukraine: House intel chair Turner
item.description: House intelligence committee chair Rep. Mike Turner said in a statement that the U.S. must think about targeting North Korean troops if they invade Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Calling out of work for your pet? City Council introduces pet sick day bill
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sick days may not just be for humans living in New York City in the future. City Councilmembers Shaun Abreu and Tiffany Cabán introduced a bill on Wednesday to let workers take sick days if their pets are ill.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: GOP maps out agenda for Trump’s first 100 days
item.description: Senate and House Republicans are setting the table for what they hope — and increasingly expect — will be unified GOP control of the White House and Congress after the election.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP celebrates 'big win' after preventing creation of new global advertising coalition
item.description: The House Judiciary Committee celebrated a “big win" this week after a Japanese company said it would not pursue a plan that would reorganize or create a new version of the advertising association that allegedly engaged in censorship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Early voting data may be sign of improved GOP ground game in pivotal swing state: expert
item.description: Michigan early voting data shows that Republicans have closed the gap on Democrats when compared to previous years, a good sign for the GOP ahead of the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris ad shows a Texas woman who lost baby and nearly died from sepsis amid strict abortion ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new series of Kamala Harris campaign ads seek to highlight increasingly perilous medical care for women since the fall of Roe v.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With dueling rallies this week, Georgia is on the mind for Trump and Harris
item.description: Both candidates are in the crucial swing state this week. Trump had two events on Wednesday, while Harris, ahead of her own visit, answered "Yes" at a CNN town hall when asked if Trump is a fascist.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: PolitiFact weighs in on false claims about Walz and how to spot disinformation
item.description: A senior U.S. intelligence official has found that Russia is behind viral disinformation about Gov. Tim Walz. An account on X spread false claims against the Democratic vice presidential nominee but a closer look at the posts reveals inconsistencies and inaccuracies.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Ngoc Bui, MPR News

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Postal Service reassures on mail ballots, says all centers operating in regions hit by hurricanes
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service has reopened all of its mail processing centers in areas of Florida and North Carolina hit by recent hurricanes, a top official said Wednesday in a briefing meant to reassure voters and election officials that the agency is ready to handle mail ballots.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Efforts by Russia, Iran and China to sway U.S. voters may escalate, new Microsoft report says
item.description: A new threat intelligence report from Microsoft says foreign adversaries have showed continued determination to influence the U.S. election and there are signs their activity will intensify as Election Day nears.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tim Walz, wife and son vote early in Minnesota
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — The Democratic vice presidential candidate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz, his wife, Gwen, and their son, Gus, went to a polling place in St. Paul on a crisp fall Wednesday morning to cast their ballots early. It was the first time voting for Gus, who just turned 18.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson's case against CNN centers on porn website's outdated software
item.description: A CNN report connects Robinson to comments, described in the suit as "lewd, sex-obsessed, racist and outrageous," posted on the porn website NudeAfrica.
item.dc:creator: Emily Mikkelsen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's former chief of staff called him a 'fascist.' Harris is campaigning on that
item.description: John Kelly, a retired Marine general who was Donald Trump's chief of staff, said his former boss fits the definition of a fascist. Now Vice President Kamala Harris is running on it.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Blinken urges Israel to seek enduring deal, as officials say a limited cease-fire has been discussed
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken says Israel needs to pursue an “enduring strategic success” in Gaza after its tactical victories against Hamas. He urged it Wednesday to seek a deal that would end the war and bring back dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: American Airlines fined $50 million for its treatment of passengers with disabilities
item.description: The U.S. Department of Transportation penalty against American Airlines comes after "numerous serious violations of the laws protecting airline passengers with disabilities" over a four-year period.
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis, MPR News

item.title: Jailed reporters, silenced networks: What Trump says he'd do to the media if elected
item.description: On the campaign trail, former President Donald Trump has threatened to jail reporters who won't give up the sources of leaks and to strip networks of their broadcast licenses for fact-checking him.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: These teachers often live in poverty. A pay raise could help — but there’s a cost
item.description: A new Biden administration effort to raise Head Start teacher wages could force the federally funded preschool program to serve fewer children.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Harris will campaign in Texas to highlight state's abortion ban in a pitch to battleground voters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will head to reliably Republican Texas just 10 days before Election Day in an effort to refocus her campaign against former President Donald Trump on reproductive care, which Democrats see as a make-or-break issue this year.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jill Douglas cites education and crime as key issues in Nevada State Senate race
item.description: Jill Douglas, a Republican running for State Senate District 6, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform as she challenges for the seat.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Harris and Trump's travel say about state of razor-thin race
item.description: Want to know where the 2024 race is being decided? Just look at where the two candidates are spending their time.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Scott Perry's Pennsylvania House race now a toss-up
item.description: Perry is in a competitive race with Democrat Janelle Stelson in a battle Cook previously had rated as "lean Republican.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Western Wisconsin race has strong implications for congressional control
item.description: Wisconsin’s third district makes up most of western Wisconsin. On the ballot this year, voters will choose between incumbent Republican Congressman Derrick Van Orden and Democrat Rebecca Cooke. The region has historically been a swing district the elected Barack Obama and later Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Eileen Eady discusses key issues in CCSD Board of Trustees race
item.description: Eileen Eady, a candidate for the open Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees seat, discussed her platform with the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to consumer safety agency’s structure
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Monday it won’t take up a case that could have delivered another blow to federal agencies. Two conservative groups sought to challenge the structure of the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), which oversees making sure the country’s consumer products are safe.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Early voting opens Monday throughout Chicago, expands to more locations in suburbs
item.description: CHICAGO — Early voting for the Nov. 5 General Election opens Monday morning throughout the city and expands to more locations around Chicagoland. Early voting in all 50 of Chicago's wards opens at 9 a.m. Monday. Any voter in the city can vote at an early voting site, no matter where they live.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Some Tampa Bay counties require vote-by-mail postage
item.description: Early voting is underway this week, and supervisors of elections also expect an increase in the return of vote-by-mail ballots.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Battleground Florida: FEMA funds
item.description: Tampa Bay is working to recover from two recent back-to-back hurricanes that impacted the state weeks away from the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, Fox 5

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5
